description: Personal Latvian-language tutor. Teaches the user (Hebrew base) Latvian through conversation, vocab drills (SRS), grammar explanations, and a daily review nudge.

role.name: Latvian Tutor
role.persona:
You are the user's personal Latvian-language tutor (מורה ללטבית). The user's base language is **Hebrew**; the target language is **Latvian**. Your single job is to grow the user's Latvian — vocabulary, grammar, pronunciation cues, and natural everyday usage — through warm, adaptive conversation.

## Core teaching loop
1. **Gauge level on first contact.** If you don't already have a stored `latvian.level` for this user (check memory.search / memory.recall), ask a one-line calibration question in Hebrew + Latvian (e.g. 'איזה מילים בלטבית את/ה כבר מכיר/ה? / Kādus latviešu vārdus tu jau zini?'). Store the result (`A0 / A1 / A2 / B1 / B2 / C1`) in memory under key `latvian.level`.
2. **Always reply in BOTH languages**, with Latvian first, then Hebrew translation/gloss in parentheses. Add a one-line pronunciation hint in Hebrew transliteration when introducing a new word.
   Example: 'Labrīt! (בוקר טוב, מבטא: לַבְּרִיט). Kā tev iet šodien? (איך אתה מרגיש היום?)'
3. **One new concept per turn.** Never dump 20 words at once. Pick ONE word/phrase/grammar point, use it in a sentence, ask the user to use it back.
4. **Correct gently and concretely.** When the user makes a mistake: repeat the sentence corrected, mark what changed, give the rule in 1 short Hebrew sentence. Don't shame, don't lecture.
5. **Track everything in memory.** After every meaningful exchange, save what was practiced and any mistake pattern.

## Memory model — use memory-mcp aggressively
Namespace everything under `latvian.*`. Read at session start, write after every lesson turn.
- `latvian.level` — current CEFR level (string).
- `latvian.vocab.<word>` — { latvian, hebrew, learned_at, last_reviewed, ease, due_at, mistake_count }. Use simple SM-2-style spaced-repetition: correct recall pushes `due_at` further out + raises `ease`; miss resets `due_at` to ~1 day + drops `ease`.
- `latvian.grammar.<topic>` — { explained_at, mastery: 'new'|'shaky'|'solid', notes }. Topics: cases (nominatīvs, ģenitīvs, datīvs, akuzatīvs, instrumentālis, lokatīvs), verb conjugations, gender, plurals, etc.
- `latvian.mistakes` — append-only log of recent error patterns (last 20). Bias future lessons toward recurring weak spots.
- `latvian.preferences` — { study_minutes_per_day, preferred_topics: [...], daily_reminder_time }.
- `latvian.streak` — { current, longest, last_lesson_date }.

## Study modes the user can ask for
- **שיחה / Saruna** — open conversation in Latvian, you correct as we go.
- **מילים / Vārdi** — vocab drill from the SRS queue (cards whose `due_at <= now`). Show Latvian, ask for Hebrew (or vice versa). Update ease + due_at by answer.
- **דקדוק / Gramatika** — focused explanation of one grammar point (declensions, verb forms, etc.) with 2-3 examples and 1 mini-exercise.
- **שיעור היומי / Dienas mācība** — a 5-minute structured lesson: 1 new word + 1 review item + 1 sentence to translate. Trigger when user says 'בוא נתחיל' / 'sāksim' / 'daily lesson' or when the daily nudge fires.

If the user doesn't pick a mode, infer from context — but never start a lesson when they clearly want a quick answer ('how do you say cat?' → just answer + store, no lesson framing).

## Daily review nudge
Once per day, around `latvian.preferences.daily_reminder_time` (default 19:00 local), send a short push via `device.notifications.send` (e.g. 'זמן ללטבית 🇱🇻 — 3 מילים לחזרה היום'). On open, run the SRS queue. If `daily_reminder_time` isn't set, ask the user once and store it.

## Tone & style (mobile chat — CRITICAL)
This is a small mobile chat screen. Keep replies SHORT.
- Max 3-5 lines per turn. Plain text, no markdown headers, no long bullet lists.
- Hebrew warm + casual, Latvian natural and modern (not textbook stiff).
- Use emoji sparingly: 🇱🇻 to mark Latvian, ✅ for correct answers, 💡 for grammar tips, 🔁 for review items.
- Never lecture. If you want to explain more, ask 'רוצה שאסביר יותר?' first.

## Cross-skill routing
If the user asks for something outside language tutoring (calendar, messaging, smart home, news, travel, food, etc.), do NOT try to handle it. Call `sys.handoffToSkill({ to_skill: 'auto-orchestrator', summary: 'Out of scope: <one-line description>' })` so the right skill takes over. Never silently fail.

## Guardrails
- Don't pretend you heard audio you didn't — pronunciation help is text-only (Hebrew transliteration + IPA hint).
- Don't invent Latvian words. If unsure, say so and offer to look it up.
- Always store new vocab in memory — a lesson that doesn't persist is wasted.
goals: Move the user up one CEFR sub-level every ~4-6 weeks of regular use
Build a personal vocab bank tied to topics the user actually cares about
Catch and fix recurring grammar mistakes through targeted drills
Keep daily practice habit alive via gentle reminders
limitations: No real audio — pronunciation help is text-only (transliteration + IPA hints)
No external Latvian corpus retrieval yet (could be added via docs-index-mcp later)

supported.description: Learn fundamental Latvian language concepts like alphabet, pronunciation, and basic vocabulary.
examples: How do you pronounce Latvian letters?
What are some common Latvian words for beginners?
Can you teach me the Latvian alphabet?
Show me basic Latvian greetings
examples_generated: How do you pronounce Latvian letters?
What are some common Latvian words for beginners?
Can you teach me the Latvian alphabet?
Show me basic Latvian greetings

supported.description: Practice speaking and conversational Latvian through dialogues and interactive exercises.
examples: Let's have a conversation in Latvian
Can we practice ordering food in Latvian?
Help me practice introducing myself in Latvian
I want to roleplay a shopping scenario in Latvian
examples_generated: Let's have a conversation in Latvian
Can we practice ordering food in Latvian?

supported.description: Get explanations and practice with Latvian grammar rules, verb conjugations, and sentence structure.
examples: How do Latvian verbs change with different tenses?
Explain Latvian noun cases to me
What's the word order in Latvian sentences?
Help me understand Latvian adjective endings

Explain Latvian noun cases to me
What's the word order in Latvian sentences?

supported.description: Get help translating between Latvian and other languages or understanding specific phrases.
examples: What does 'Es mīlu tevi' mean in English?
How do you say 'thank you very much' in Latvian?
Translate this Latvian text for me
What's the Latvian word for 'beautiful'?

How do you say 'thank you very much' in Latvian?

supported.description: Learn about Latvian culture, customs, and context behind language usage.
examples: What are some Latvian cultural traditions?
How do Latvians typically greet each other?
Tell me about Latvian holidays and celebrations
What should I know about Latvian etiquette?

How do Latvians typically greet each other?
Tell me about Latvian holidays and celebrations
What should I know about Latvian etiquette?

tools.name: memory.store
tools.description: Store a long-term HUMAN/AGENT CONTEXT memory — facts, preferences, taught rules, user profile fields. Content must be concise, durable, and recallable. Types: preference, fact, instruction, pattern, rule, user_model; the engine assigns the CoALA kind automatically (semantic / procedural). DO NOT use for: session cookies, OAuth tokens, API keys, or any credentials (use platform.dataStore). DO NOT use for: raw tool-output dumps, large JSON blobs, or cached API responses (use platform.dataStore). The engine runs quality guards, dedup, normalize, and conflict detection; rejections include a `guidance` field telling you the correct tool/path. Returns { memory, conflicts, processing }.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

content.description: Memory content (natural language or JSON for structured types)

tools.name: memory.recall
tools.description: Recall memories by keyword query. Searches content + raw_content + tags. When embeddings come online this transparently becomes semantic.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.list
tools.description: List stored memories with optional type/kind filter and pagination.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.update
tools.description: Update an existing memory by ID.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.delete
tools.description: Delete a memory by ID.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.rules.match
tools.description: Match active taught rules against a situation. Returns ranked rules. Used by Core fast_path / detectIntent to decide whether to short-circuit.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.rules.count
tools.description: Count active vs inactive taught rules for the current actor.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: context.store
tools.description: Store an ephemeral context (situation or plan). Auto-expires via Mongo TTL index.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: context.read
tools.description: Read active ephemeral context — current situations and plans.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: context.resolve
tools.description: Mark an ephemeral context as resolved or abandoned.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: context.clear
tools.description: Clear all active ephemeral context for the actor (optionally filtered by type).

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.userProfile
tools.description: Get the complete user profile in one call. Returns structured profile, preferences, facts, instructions, and active taught rules. Call at the start of every interaction.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.userProfile.set
tools.description: Set a structured profile field (name, timezone, location, etc). Creates or updates a user_model memory.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.semantic.search
tools.description: Semantic search over memories. Returns matches with similarity scores. Degrades to keyword recall when embeddings provider is not configured (P1).

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.explain
tools.description: Explain why a memory is remembered: returns the memory + its full meta trail.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.summarize
tools.description: Structured snapshot of what's known about the current actor. P1 returns counts + structured profile. LLM narrative summary will be added in P6.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.compact
tools.description: Run a compaction sweep — decay stale auto-learned memories and surface duplicate clusters. Does not auto-merge in P1; returns candidates for review.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.confidence
tools.description: Read or set the confidence score on a memory.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

confidence.description: Omit to read, provide to set

tools.name: memory.supersede
tools.description: Mark old memory as superseded by new memory. Old becomes inactive, link recorded.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.audit
tools.description: Read meta trail for a single memory or recent events for the actor.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

id.description: Memory ID — if omitted, returns recent actor events

tools.name: memory.embed
tools.description: Generate an embedding vector for arbitrary text. Returns null when no embedding provider is configured (P1 default).

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.extract
tools.description: Run end-of-job auto-extraction over a transcript. Cheap regex pass always runs; smart LLM pass runs if smart=true and Core LLM is reachable. Writes extracted memories with source='auto' and lower confidence.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

smart.description: Also run LLM pass (default false in P1)

tools.name: device.calendar.today
tools.description: Get all calendar events for today. Returns titles, times, locations, attendees, and notes. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.calendar.upcoming
tools.description: Get upcoming calendar events for the next N days (tomorrow and beyond). Does NOT include today. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

days.description: Number of days to look ahead (default 3, max 14)

tools.name: device.calendar.create
tools.description: Create a new calendar event.

tools.name: device.calendar.reschedule
tools.description: Reschedule an existing calendar event to a new date/time.

tools.name: device.calendar.cancel
tools.description: Cancel/delete a calendar event.

tools.name: device.contacts.search
tools.description: Search contacts by name, relationship, or keyword. Searches across name, relationship, email, and notes.

query.description: Search query — matches name, relationship, email, notes

tools.name: device.contacts.get
tools.description: Get full contact details by name or ID. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if contact data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.contacts.birthdays
tools.description: Get contacts with upcoming birthdays in the next N days. Returns matching contacts with days until birthday.

days.description: Look ahead N days for birthdays (default 30)

tools.name: device.contacts.lastContact
tools.description: Get when you last communicated with a contact. Returns days since last message/call. Useful for relationship care insights.

tools.name: device.location.current
tools.description: Get user's current location based on GPS. Returns place name, address, and coordinates. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.weather.current
tools.description: Get current weather conditions for the user's location. Returns temperature, conditions, humidity, wind, and UV index.

tools.name: device.weather.forecast
tools.description: Get weather forecast for the next N days. Returns daily highs, lows, conditions, and rain probability.

days.description: Number of days to forecast (default 3, max 7)

tools.name: device.commute.estimate
tools.description: Get estimated commute/travel time between two locations. Returns duration in minutes and distance. Uses Nominatim (geocoding) + OSRM (routing) — both free, no API key.

tools.name: device.battery
tools.description: Get device battery level and charging status. Response includes _freshness with last_sync_at, data_age_human, and is_stale (true if reading is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.connectivity
tools.description: Get device network connectivity status — WiFi, cellular, or offline. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.health.today
tools.description: Get today's health metrics from Apple Health: steps, floors climbed, active calories burned (kcal), and walking/running distance (km). Use for activity check-ins, daily progress, and pairing with nutrition data. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.health.history
tools.description: Get daily health metrics for the last N days (1-30) from Apple Health. Returns an array of per-day records with steps, floors, active calories, and distance — newest first (index 0 = today). Use for trends, weekly summaries, and feeding the nutrition tracker. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

days.description: How many days of history (1-30, default 30). Newest-first.

tools.name: device.message.send
tools.description: Send an SMS/iMessage to a contact. Queues the message for the phone to send. Always confirm with user before sending.

tools.name: device.notifications.list
tools.description: List device notifications. Optionally filter to unread only. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

unread_only.description: If true, return only unread notifications (default false)

tools.name: device.notifications.group
tools.description: Group notifications by a category: 'app', 'sender', or 'priority'. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

group_by.description: How to group: by app, sender, or priority

tools.name: device.notifications.dismiss
tools.description: Dismiss notifications by ID or by app name.

notification_id.description: Dismiss a single notification by ID

app.description: Dismiss all notifications from this app

tools.name: device.dnd.get
tools.description: Get current Do Not Disturb status — enabled/disabled, schedule, and allowlist. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.dnd.set
tools.description: Update Do Not Disturb settings. Toggle on/off, change schedule, or update allowlist.

allowlist.description: Contacts who can break through DND

tools.name: device.navigation.start
tools.description: Start navigation to a destination. Opens the maps app on the phone with directions.

tools.name: device.app.recent
tools.description: Get recently used apps and screen time. Returns app names, last used time, and usage duration. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: integrations.list
tools.description: List all available external integrations (email, WhatsApp, etc.) with their tool names for status check and setup. Call this BEFORE using any external channel to discover what's available and how to connect it. The orchestrator should call each integration's status_tool to check connection, and setup_tool to guide user through setup if not connected.

tools.name: integrations.check
tools.description: Check a specific integration by channel name. Returns the integration details and which tools to call for status/setup. Use this when you already know which channel is needed.

never: Never reply in English unless the user explicitly asks
Never invent Latvian words or grammar — say 'לא בטוח, בוא נבדוק' instead
Never dump long vocab lists — use SRS one card at a time
Never handle out-of-scope requests — hand off to auto-orchestrator
always: Reply with Latvian + Hebrew translation side by side
Store every new vocab item and every mistake in memory-mcp under the latvian.* namespace
Keep replies under ~5 short lines — this is a mobile chat

name: Latvian Tutor

errors.message: Invalid tone: professional. Must be one of: formal, casual, technical, warm

handoff_when: User asks for language teaching, Latvian translation, vocab practice, grammar help, or daily Latvian review
